content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about your life, interests, and future plans.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about yourself, your experiences, and your ambitions. Give extended answers with reasons, examples, and personal reflections.
examiner_questions: Tell me about where you live. What do you like about it?
What do you enjoy doing in your spare time? Has this changed over the years?
What kind of music or films do you like? Can you explain why?
What are your plans for the future? What career are you interested in?
If you could visit any country in the world, where would you go and why?
content.sample_answer: I live in a medium-sized city in southern China. What I like most about it is that it has a good balance between modern facilities and natural beauty — there are shopping centres and cinemas, but also mountains and a river nearby. In my spare time, I enjoy playing the piano and reading novels. When I was younger, I used to play a lot of video games, but now I find reading more enjoyable because it helps me relax and learn at the same time. I'm really into science fiction films because they make me think about what the future could look like. In the future, I'd love to study computer science at university because I'm fascinated by artificial intelligence. If I could visit any country, I'd choose Japan because I'm interested in their technology and culture.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分要求更高水平的回答。技巧：1）使用丰富的词汇和复杂句式；2）谈论变化和对比（过去和现在）；3）使用虚拟语气（If I could.../ I would...）；4）展示反思能力，解释你的想法背后的原因；5）自然地扩展回答，但不要偏离话题。语法准确性和词汇多样性是FCE评分的重要标准。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分。考官会问你关于生活、兴趣和未来计划的问题。与PET相比，FCE要求更深入、更有思考深度的回答，需要展示复杂的语法结构和丰富的词汇。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1. The examiner asks about your life, interests, and future plans. Compared to PET, FCE requires deeper, more thoughtful answers that demonstrate complex grammar structures and a wide range of vocabulary.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about your work or studies and how you spend your time.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your studies, daily routine, and learning experiences. Give detailed answers with examples and explanations.
examiner_questions: Tell me about what you are studying at the moment. What do you enjoy most about it?
How do you usually organise your time for studying? Do you have any particular methods?
Do you think the education system in your country is effective? Why or why not?
What is the most challenging thing you have ever had to learn? How did you overcome the difficulty?
If you could change one thing about your school or college, what would it be?
content.sample_answer: At the moment, I'm in my final year of senior high school, and I'm studying a range of subjects including English, maths, and physics. I enjoy English the most because it opens up a whole world of literature and communication. When it comes to organising my time, I tend to make a detailed study plan at the beginning of each week, which helps me stay on track and avoid last-minute cramming. I think the education system here has both strengths and weaknesses — it's very thorough in terms of academic knowledge, but there could be more emphasis on creativity and critical thinking. The most challenging thing I've learned was probably advanced mathematics, but I overcame the difficulty by watching online tutorials and practising consistently. If I could change one thing about my school, I would introduce more project-based learning, as I believe it would help students apply their knowledge to real-world situations.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于学习和工作的话题。技巧：1）不只是描述事实，还要分析和评价（I think... because...）；2）使用过去和现在的对比来展示时态变化；3）使用条件句和虚拟语气（If I could change...）；4）提供具体例子来支持你的观点；5）展示批判性思维，不只是说好的一面。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕学习和工作。考官想了解你的学习经历、方法和对教育的看法。回答时要展示你的分析能力和批判性思维。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on work and study. The examiner wants to understand your learning experiences, methods, and views on education. Show analytical ability and critical thinking in your answers.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about your social life and relationships.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your friendships, social activities, and how you interact with others. Give thoughtful answers with reasons and reflections.
examiner_questions: How important are your friends to you? What qualities do you value most in a friend?
Do you prefer spending time with a large group of friends or with one or two close friends? Why?
How has social media affected the way people maintain friendships, in your experience?
Can you describe a memorable occasion you spent with friends or family?
Do you think it is important to have friends from different backgrounds and cultures? Why?
content.sample_answer: My friends are extremely important to me — I would say they are one of the most valuable parts of my life. The qualities I value most are honesty and a good sense of humour, because I believe that true friends should be able to be themselves around each other. I generally prefer spending time with one or two close friends rather than in a large group, as I find the conversations more meaningful and personal. In terms of social media, it has definitely made it easier to stay in touch with people, especially those who live far away, but I sometimes worry that online communication is replacing genuine face-to-face interaction. One of my most memorable experiences was a hiking trip with my closest friends last summer — we spent three days in the mountains, and it really strengthened our bond. I absolutely think it's important to have friends from different backgrounds because it broadens your perspective and helps you become more open-minded.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于社交生活的话题。技巧：1）使用丰富的形容词来描述人和关系（honest, supportive, open-minded）；2）表达偏好时给出充分的理由（I prefer... because...）；3）讨论社交媒体等现代话题时展示平衡的观点；4）用具体的个人经历来支持你的观点；5）使用高级表达如 "I would say"、"In terms of"、"it has definitely" 来增加流利度。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕社交生活和人际关系。考官想了解你的交友观、社交偏好和对现代社交方式的看法。要展示你能用英语流利地讨论人际关系话题。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on social life and relationships. The examiner wants to understand your views on friendship, social preferences, and modern social interaction. Show you can discuss interpersonal topics fluently in English.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about culture, entertainment, and your cultural interests.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your cultural interests, favourite forms of entertainment, and how culture influences your life. Give extended answers with specific examples.
examiner_questions: What kind of books do you enjoy reading? Has your taste in books changed over time?
Do you think it is important for young people to learn about their own culture and traditions? Why?
How do you usually find out about new films, music, or TV shows?
Have you ever been to a live performance, such as a concert or a play? What was it like?
Do you think the internet has made it easier or harder to appreciate different cultures? Explain your view.
content.sample_answer: I really enjoy reading fiction, particularly historical novels, because they allow me to learn about the past while being entertained by a compelling story. When I was younger, I mostly read fantasy books, but as I've grown older, I've developed an appreciation for more realistic and thought-provoking literature. I strongly believe it's important for young people to learn about their own culture and traditions, as it gives them a sense of identity and helps them understand where they come from. I usually discover new entertainment through recommendations from friends or through online platforms. Last year, I went to a classical music concert for the first time, and it was an absolutely breathtaking experience — the atmosphere in the concert hall was completely different from listening to music at home. As for the internet, I think it has mostly made it easier to appreciate different cultures, as you can access art, music, and literature from around the world with just a few clicks.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于文化兴趣的话题。技巧：1）展示你的文化素养和广泛兴趣；2）描述变化和发展（When I was younger... but now...）；3）使用感官描写来描述体验（breathtaking, atmosphere）；4）对抽象话题如文化的重要性表达有深度的看法；5）使用强调表达如 "I strongly believe"、"absolutely" 增加表达力度。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕文化和娱乐。考官想了解你的文化兴趣和对文化相关话题的看法。展示你的文化素养和对不同艺术形式的理解。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on culture and entertainment. The examiner wants to understand your cultural interests and views on culture-related topics. Demonstrate cultural awareness and understanding of different art forms.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about health, fitness, and your lifestyle.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your health habits, fitness activities, and views on healthy living. Give detailed answers with personal examples and opinions.
examiner_questions: What do you do to stay healthy? Has your approach to health changed recently?
Do you think young people today are more or less health-conscious than previous generations? Why?
How important is it to have a balanced diet? Do you find it easy or difficult to eat healthily?
What is your favourite form of exercise, and why do you enjoy it?
Do you think the government should do more to promote healthy lifestyles? What could they do?
content.sample_answer: To stay healthy, I try to exercise regularly and eat a balanced diet. Recently, I've started running in the mornings, which has made a noticeable difference to my energy levels throughout the day. I think young people today are actually more health-conscious in some ways — many of my friends go to the gym and pay attention to nutrition — but at the same time, we spend far too much time sitting in front of screens. Eating healthily is quite important to me, although I must admit I find it challenging when I'm busy with schoolwork, as it's tempting to just grab fast food. My favourite form of exercise is swimming because it works the whole body and I find it very relaxing. Regarding the government's role, I believe they should invest more in public sports facilities and promote health education in schools, because prevention is always better than cure.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于健康和生活方式的话题。技巧：1）使用健康相关的专业词汇（balanced diet, nutrition, health-conscious）；2）承认事情的复杂性（in some ways... but at the same time...）；3）诚实地表达困难和挑战（I must admit I find it challenging）；4）提出建设性的建议来展示思考深度；5）使用谚语或常用表达如 "prevention is better than cure" 展示语言积累。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕健康和生活方式。考官想了解你的健康习惯和对健康生活的看法。要展示你能使用健康相关词汇，并能对健康话题进行有深度的讨论。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on health and lifestyle. The examiner wants to understand your health habits and views on healthy living. Show you can use health-related vocabulary and discuss health topics in depth.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about technology and how it affects your daily life.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your use of technology, your opinions on its impact, and how it has changed the way people live. Give thoughtful, extended answers.
examiner_questions: How important is technology in your daily life? What devices do you use most?
Do you think people have become too dependent on technology? Can you give examples?
How has technology changed the way people communicate with each other?
What do you think is the most significant technological invention of the last twenty years? Why?
Are there any aspects of technology that worry you? What concerns do you have?
content.sample_answer: Technology plays an enormous role in my daily life — I use my smartphone for virtually everything, from studying and communicating to entertainment and navigation. I do think there's a risk of becoming too dependent on technology. For example, many people can no longer read a map or do basic calculations without their phone, which is somewhat concerning. Technology has fundamentally changed communication — we can now reach anyone instantly, but this convenience has come at the cost of face-to-face interaction. I would say the smartphone is probably the most significant invention of the last twenty years because it has combined so many tools into a single device and completely transformed how we live. However, I do have some concerns about technology, particularly regarding privacy and data security. The amount of personal information that companies collect about us is quite alarming, and I think there need to be stronger regulations to protect people's digital rights.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于科技的话题。技巧：1）展示对科技利弊的平衡看法；2）使用科技相关词汇（dependent, privacy, data security, regulations）；3）提供具体例子来支持观点；4）表达担忧时使用适当的语气缓和词（somewhat, quite, I think）；5）使用高级句式如 "this convenience has come at the cost of" 展示语言水平。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕科技及其对日常生活的影响。考官想了解你对科技的态度和看法。要展示你能平衡地讨论科技的利弊，使用相关专业词汇。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on technology and its impact on daily life. The examiner wants to understand your attitudes and views on technology. Show you can discuss the pros and cons of technology in a balanced way using relevant vocabulary.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about your plans and ambitions for the future.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your hopes, plans, and dreams for the future. Give extended answers showing reflection and forward thinking.
examiner_questions: Where do you see yourself in ten years' time? What would you like to be doing?
Is there a particular skill or qualification you would like to gain in the future? Why?
Do you think it is important to set goals for the future? How do you plan for your future?
Would you prefer to live and work in your own country or abroad? What are the advantages of each?
What kind of world do you hope future generations will live in?
content.sample_answer: In ten years' time, I would like to have finished university and started a career in environmental engineering. I'm passionate about sustainability, so I hope to be working on projects that make a real difference to the environment. I would really like to gain a master's degree, ideally at a university abroad, because I think studying in a different country broadens your horizons enormously. I'm a firm believer in setting goals — I find that having clear objectives helps me stay motivated and focused. As for where I'd like to live, I think there are advantages to both options. Living abroad would expose me to new cultures and ideas, but staying in my own country would allow me to contribute to its development directly. Ultimately, I hope future generations will live in a more sustainable and equitable world, where technology is used to solve problems rather than create them. It may sound idealistic, but I believe small actions today can lead to significant changes tomorrow.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于未来计划的话题。技巧：1）使用将来时和虚拟语气表达希望和计划（I would like to, I hope to）；2）展示对未来的深度思考，不只是简单的计划；3）使用高级表达如 "I'm a firm believer in"、"broaden your horizons"；4）讨论不同选择时展示分析能力；5）表达理想主义的同时保持现实感（It may sound idealistic, but...）。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕未来计划和抱负。考官想了解你对未来的思考和规划。要展示你能用英语表达对未来的展望，包括职业目标和个人理想。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on future plans and ambitions. The examiner wants to understand your thinking and planning for the future. Show you can express future aspirations in English, including career goals and personal ideals.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about media, news, and your community.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about how you consume media, your involvement in your community, and your views on current issues. Give detailed, thoughtful responses.
examiner_questions: How do you usually keep up with the news? Do you prefer traditional media or online sources?
Do you think the news we see on social media is always reliable? Why or why not?
Are you involved in any community activities or clubs? What do you gain from them?
What do you think makes a strong and supportive community?
If you could do something to improve your local community, what would you do?
content.sample_answer: I mainly keep up with the news through online sources and social media, as they are more convenient and provide information in real time. However, I do read newspaper articles occasionally because I find they tend to offer more in-depth analysis. I don't think news on social media is always reliable — anyone can post anything, and misinformation spreads very quickly. It's crucial to check multiple sources before believing something. I'm a member of a volunteer group at my school that organises community clean-up events and visits to elderly care homes. I gain a great deal from these activities — not only practical skills but also a sense of belonging and purpose. I think a strong community is built on mutual respect, communication, and a willingness to help one another. If I could improve my local community, I would set up a neighbourhood library and community garden, as I believe these spaces bring people together and create a stronger sense of connection.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于媒体和社区的话题。技巧：1）讨论媒体时展示批判性思维（check multiple sources, misinformation）；2）使用对比来展示不同媒体的优劣；3）讨论社区活动时展示你的社会责任感；4）使用条件句表达改善建议（If I could... I would...）；5）使用高级词汇如 "mutual respect"、"sense of belonging"、"misinformation" 展示B2水平。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕媒体、新闻和社区。考官想了解你获取信息的方式和对社区的看法。要展示你的批判性思维和社会意识。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on media, news, and community. The examiner wants to understand how you consume information and your views on community. Demonstrate critical thinking and social awareness.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about travel, holidays, and exploring new places.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your travel experiences, holiday preferences, and views on the value of travel. Give extended answers with personal anecdotes.
examiner_questions: Do you enjoy travelling? What kind of holidays do you prefer?
Tell me about a place you have visited that made a strong impression on you. Why was it memorable?
Do you think travelling is the best way to learn about other cultures? Are there other ways?
Would you rather explore your own country or travel abroad? Why?
How has tourism changed the places people visit? Is this a positive or negative development?
content.sample_answer: I absolutely love travelling — it's one of my greatest passions. I tend to prefer trips that combine sightseeing with outdoor activities, such as hiking or cycling, because I find that you experience a place much more deeply when you explore it actively. The most memorable place I've visited was Xi'an, where I saw the Terracotta Warriors for the first time. Standing in front of thousands of life-sized clay soldiers that are over two thousand years old was genuinely awe-inspiring. While I do think travelling is an excellent way to learn about other cultures, it's not the only way — reading books, watching documentaries, and making friends from different backgrounds can also deepen your understanding considerably. I enjoy both domestic and international travel, but if I had to choose, I'd say exploring my own country first is important because many people know more about foreign destinations than their own homeland. As for tourism's impact, it's a double-edged sword — it brings economic benefits but can also damage the environment and change the character of a place.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于旅行的话题。技巧：1）使用生动的描述语言来讲述旅行经历（awe-inspiring, deeply）；2）表达偏好时给出充分的理由；3）讨论旅行的价值时展示全面的思考；4）使用习语如 "double-edged sword" 展示语言积累；5）用具体的地名和经历增加回答的真实感。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕旅行和探索新地方。考官想了解你的旅行经历和对旅行价值的看法。要展示你能用丰富的语言描述旅行体验，并能讨论旅游业的影响。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on travel and exploring new places. The examiner wants to understand your travel experiences and views on the value of travel. Show you can describe travel experiences vividly and discuss tourism's impact.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about the environment and sustainability.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about environmental issues, your personal habits, and what can be done to protect the planet. Give thoughtful answers with examples.
examiner_questions: How concerned are you about environmental issues? Which issue worries you most?
What do you do in your daily life to help the environment?
Do you think young people care more about the environment than older generations? Why?
What do you think is the biggest obstacle to solving environmental problems?
If you could make one change to help the environment, what would it be?
content.sample_answer: I'm quite concerned about environmental issues, particularly climate change, because its effects are becoming increasingly visible and the window for meaningful action is narrowing. In my daily life, I try to reduce waste by using reusable bags and water bottles, and I walk or cycle whenever possible instead of taking the car. I do think young people tend to care more about the environment — partly because we're the generation that will deal with the consequences most directly, and partly because environmental education has become much more prominent in schools. The biggest obstacle, in my opinion, is short-term economic thinking. Many governments and businesses prioritise immediate profits over long-term sustainability, which makes it extremely difficult to implement the fundamental changes that are needed. If I could make one change, I would introduce a global carbon tax, because I believe economic incentives are the most effective way to drive large-scale behavioural change.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于环境的话题。技巧：1）展示你对环境问题的了解和关注；2）用具体的个人行动来展示你的环保意识；3）讨论代际差异时展示分析能力；4）提出深层次的障碍分析（short-term economic thinking）；5）使用条件句提出有创意的解决方案。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕环境和可持续发展。考官想了解你对环境问题的看法和个人行动。展示你的环保意识和对环境议题的深入思考。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on the environment and sustainability. The examiner wants to understand your views on environmental issues and personal actions. Show environmental awareness and deep thinking about ecological topics.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about learning languages and cross-cultural communication.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your language learning experiences, the challenges of communication, and the value of multilingualism. Give extended, reflective answers.
examiner_questions: Why did you decide to learn English? How long have you been studying it?
What do you find most difficult about learning a foreign language?
Do you think everyone should learn at least one foreign language? Why?
Have you ever had a misunderstanding because of a language or cultural difference?
How do you think learning a language changes the way you think?
content.sample_answer: I started learning English when I was about seven years old, initially because it was a school requirement, but over time I developed a genuine love for the language. The most difficult aspect for me has been improving my speaking fluency — I can read and write quite well, but expressing myself spontaneously in conversation is still challenging. I absolutely believe everyone should learn at least one foreign language because it opens up so many opportunities and helps you understand different ways of thinking. I once had an amusing misunderstanding with an English-speaking friend when I used a Chinese idiom directly translated into English — it made no sense at all, and we both ended up laughing. Learning English has definitely changed the way I think — I've noticed that I sometimes approach problems differently depending on which language I'm thinking in, which is a fascinating experience.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于语言学习的话题。技巧：1）分享真实的语言学习经历和挑战；2）展示对多语言能力价值的深入理解；3）用幽默的个人故事来展示你的沟通能力；4）讨论语言与思维的关系来展示深度思考；5）使用高级表达如 "spontaneously"、"fascinating experience" 展示语言水平。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕语言学习和跨文化交流。考官想了解你的语言学习经历和对多语言能力的看法。展示你的语言学习反思和跨文化意识。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on language learning and cross-cultural communication. The examiner wants to understand your language learning experiences and views on multilingualism. Show language learning reflection and cross-cultural awareness.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about food, cooking, and dining habits.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your food preferences, cooking experiences, and views on food culture. Give detailed answers with personal examples.
examiner_questions: What kind of food do you enjoy most? Has your taste changed as you've grown older?
Do you know how to cook? How did you learn?
Do you think fast food is a serious problem in modern society? Why or why not?
How important is it to eat together as a family? Is this common in your country?
If you could try any cuisine from around the world, which would you choose and why?
content.sample_answer: I enjoy a wide variety of food, but I'm particularly fond of traditional Sichuan cuisine because of its bold, spicy flavours. My taste has definitely evolved over the years — when I was younger, I was quite a fussy eater, but now I'm much more adventurous and willing to try new things. I can cook basic dishes — my mother taught me the essentials during lockdown, and I discovered that cooking can actually be quite therapeutic. I do think fast food is a concern because it's so convenient and affordable that many people, especially students, rely on it heavily, which has obvious health consequences. Eating together as a family is still quite common in China and I think it's extremely important — it's not just about the food but about communication, connection, and maintaining family bonds. If I could try any cuisine, I'd choose Peruvian food, because I've heard it combines influences from many different cultures, which sounds absolutely fascinating.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于食物的话题。技巧：1）使用丰富的食物相关词汇（bold flavours, fussy eater, adventurous）；2）描述变化过程（When I was younger... but now...）；3）讨论社会问题时展示平衡的观点；4）解释文化习俗时提供文化背景；5）用好奇心和热情来回答最后一个问题。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕食物、烹饪和饮食习惯。考官想了解你的饮食偏好和对食物文化的看法。展示你能用丰富的词汇讨论食物话题。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on food, cooking, and dining habits. The examiner wants to understand your food preferences and views on food culture. Show you can discuss food topics with rich vocabulary.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about creativity, art, and self-expression.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about creative activities, your views on art, and how you express yourself. Give thoughtful, extended answers.
examiner_questions: Do you consider yourself a creative person? In what ways?
What role does art play in your life? Do you have any artistic hobbies?
Do you think creativity can be taught, or is it something you're born with?
How important is it for schools to encourage creativity in students?
What is the most creative thing you have ever done?
content.sample_answer: I'd like to think I'm reasonably creative, although my creativity tends to come out more in writing and problem-solving than in traditional art forms. I enjoy creative writing in my spare time — I find that putting my thoughts into stories helps me understand my own emotions and experiences better. I believe creativity is a combination of natural talent and practice. Some people may have a natural inclination, but creativity can absolutely be developed and nurtured through exposure to different ideas and regular practice. I think it's vital for schools to encourage creativity because it teaches students to think flexibly and approach challenges from multiple angles — skills that are increasingly valued in the modern workplace. The most creative thing I've done was probably writing and directing a short film for a school competition. It was an incredibly challenging but rewarding experience that taught me the importance of collaboration and perseverance.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于创造力的话题。技巧：1）用具体例子来定义你的创造力；2）讨论 "天赋vs后天" 时展示思考深度；3）将创造力与实际技能和职业价值联系起来；4）分享具体的创意项目来展示你的经验；5）使用高级表达如 "natural inclination"、"nurtured through" 展示语言水平。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕创造力和自我表达。考官想了解你对创造力的看法和创意活动的经历。展示你能深入讨论抽象话题如创造力的本质。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on creativity and self-expression. The examiner wants to understand your views on creativity and creative activities. Show you can discuss abstract topics like the nature of creativity in depth.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about global issues and your awareness of the world.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about global challenges, international awareness, and your views on the interconnected world. Give detailed, thoughtful responses.
examiner_questions: What global issue do you think deserves more attention? Why?
Do you think young people today are more aware of global issues than previous generations?
How do you stay informed about what's happening in other countries?
Do you think individuals can make a difference to global problems, or is it up to governments?
What do you think the world will look like in fifty years?
content.sample_answer: I think mental health deserves far more attention globally. Despite increasing awareness, there's still a significant stigma in many cultures, and access to mental health services remains inadequate in most countries. I do believe young people today are more globally aware, largely thanks to social media and the internet, which expose us to issues that previous generations might never have known about. I stay informed through a combination of news apps, podcasts, and following international journalists on social media. As for whether individuals can make a difference, I think the answer is yes, but within limits. Individual actions create momentum and raise awareness, but systemic problems ultimately require government-level solutions. Looking fifty years ahead, I imagine a world that is more technologically advanced and interconnected, but also facing greater environmental challenges. I hope we'll have made significant progress on renewable energy and global cooperation, but I'm cautiously optimistic rather than blindly hopeful.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于全球问题的话题。技巧：1）选择具体的全球问题并给出充分的理由；2）比较不同代际的全球意识时使用因果分析；3）展示你获取国际新闻的多种渠道；4）讨论个人vs政府责任时展示平衡思维；5）展望未来时保持批判性和现实感（cautiously optimistic）。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕全球问题和国际意识。考官想了解你对世界事务的关注和看法。展示你的全球视野和对国际问题的深入思考。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on global issues and world awareness. The examiner wants to understand your engagement with world affairs. Show global perspective and deep thinking about international issues.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about sport, competition, and teamwork.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your experiences with sport and competition, and your views on the role of sport in society. Give extended answers with reasons.
examiner_questions: Do you enjoy playing or watching sport? Which sports interest you most?
Do you think sport is given too much or too little importance in schools?
What have you learned from participating in competitive activities?
Do you think individual sports or team sports are more beneficial? Why?
How do you think sport brings people together?
content.sample_answer: I enjoy both playing and watching sport, though I tend to participate more in individual activities like badminton and running. I find team sports exciting to watch, particularly basketball, because the combination of individual skill and team strategy is fascinating. I think sport is given about the right amount of importance in schools, although I would argue that schools should offer a wider variety of sports so that students who aren't interested in traditional games like football can still find something they enjoy. From competitive activities, I've learned valuable lessons about resilience, discipline, and handling pressure. I think both individual and team sports are beneficial but in different ways — individual sports teach self-reliance and personal responsibility, while team sports develop communication and cooperation skills. Sport has a remarkable ability to bring people together because it transcends language, culture, and social barriers. When you're supporting the same team or playing together, differences seem to fade away.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于运动的话题。技巧：1）区分参与和观看运动的不同体验；2）提出建设性的改进建议（wider variety of sports）；3）从竞争中提取人生经验教训；4）比较个人运动和团队运动时要公平全面；5）使用高级表达如 "transcends"、"self-reliance" 展示语言水平。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕运动和竞争。考官想了解你的运动经历和对运动在社会中作用的看法。展示你能从运动中提炼出更广泛的人生教训。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on sport and competition. The examiner wants to understand your sporting experiences and views on sport's role in society. Show you can draw broader life lessons from sport.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about money, shopping, and consumer habits.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your attitudes towards money, your shopping habits, and views on consumerism. Give thoughtful answers with examples.
examiner_questions: Do you think young people today understand the value of money? Why or why not?
How do you usually decide whether to buy something? What factors do you consider?
Do you prefer shopping online or in physical shops? What are the advantages of each?
Do you think advertising influences what young people buy? How?
If you were given a large amount of money, what would you do with it?
content.sample_answer: I think many young people understand the value of money in theory, but since most of us haven't had to earn a living independently yet, it can be hard to fully appreciate what things cost. When deciding whether to buy something, I usually consider whether I genuinely need it, whether the quality justifies the price, and whether I could find a better deal elsewhere. I must confess I do most of my shopping online because it's incredibly convenient and you can compare prices easily, but I still enjoy visiting physical shops for clothes because I like to try things on before buying. Advertising undoubtedly influences young people's purchasing decisions — social media advertising is particularly effective because it feels more personal and less obvious than traditional adverts. If I were given a large sum of money, I would invest most of it in my education — perhaps studying abroad — and donate a portion to an environmental charity, because I believe in using resources to create lasting positive impact.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于金钱和消费的话题。技巧：1）诚实地分析年轻人与金钱的关系；2）描述购物决策过程来展示理性思维；3）比较线上和线下购物时要具体；4）讨论广告影响时展示批判性意识；5）回答假设问题时展示价值观（education, charity）。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕金钱和消费习惯。考官想了解你对金钱的态度和消费观。展示你的理性思维和对消费文化的批判性理解。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on money and consumer habits. The examiner wants to understand your attitudes towards money and consumption. Show rational thinking and critical understanding of consumer culture.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about home life, family, and growing up.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your family life, upbringing, and the role of family in modern society. Give extended answers with personal reflections.
examiner_questions: Can you tell me about your family? What role does your family play in your life?
Has family life changed compared to your grandparents' generation? How?
What traditions does your family follow? Are they important to you?
Do you think parents should be strict or relaxed with their children? Why?
What is the most important thing your family has taught you?
content.sample_answer: My family is quite close-knit — I live with my parents, and my grandparents live nearby, so we see each other regularly. My family plays a central role in my life, providing emotional support and guidance, especially during stressful times like exam periods. Family life has changed considerably compared to my grandparents' generation. Families tend to be smaller now, and both parents usually work, which means there's less time spent together at home. We follow several family traditions, particularly during the Spring Festival, when the whole extended family gathers for a reunion dinner. These traditions are very important to me because they strengthen our bonds and connect us to our cultural heritage. I think parents should find a balance between strictness and freedom — too much control can stifle a child's independence, but too little can leave them without necessary boundaries. The most important thing my family has taught me is the value of perseverance — my parents always encouraged me to keep trying, even when things seemed impossible.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于家庭生活的话题。技巧：1）描述家庭关系时使用情感词汇（close-knit, emotional support）；2）比较不同代际的家庭生活时注意文化变化；3）讨论家庭传统时展示文化意识；4）在 "严格vs宽松" 的讨论中寻找平衡点；5）分享家庭教会你的人生经验来展示成熟度。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕家庭和成长。考官想了解你的家庭生活和对家庭在现代社会中角色的看法。展示你的家庭价值观和文化意识。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on home life and family. The examiner wants to understand your family life and views on the role of family in modern society. Show family values and cultural awareness.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about career development and the world of work.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about career aspirations, work experience, and the changing nature of employment. Give detailed, thoughtful responses.
examiner_questions: What kind of job would you like to have in the future? Why does it appeal to you?
Have you ever had any work experience, such as a part-time job or internship?
What skills do you think are most important for success in the workplace?
How do you think the world of work will change in the next twenty years?
Is it more important to enjoy your job or to earn a high salary?
content.sample_answer: I'd love to work in the field of renewable energy, possibly as an engineer or project manager. It appeals to me because it combines my interest in science with my passion for environmental sustainability — I want my career to have a positive impact on the world. I haven't had a formal job yet, but I did a two-week internship at a local technology company last summer, which gave me valuable insight into how businesses operate. I think the most important skills for the workplace are adaptability and communication. Technology is changing so rapidly that the ability to learn new skills quickly is essential, and strong communication ensures you can work effectively with others. I think the world of work will become increasingly flexible and remote, with AI automating many routine tasks. This will create new opportunities but also require workers to continuously upskill. As for salary versus enjoyment, I believe enjoyment should come first, because you spend such a large proportion of your life working that doing something you hate would be genuinely miserable, regardless of how much you earn.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于职业发展的话题。技巧：1）将职业选择与个人价值观联系起来；2）分享实习或工作经验时提取关键学习点；3）讨论职场技能时要有前瞻性；4）展望未来工作变化时要具体（AI, remote work）；5）在 "薪水vs兴趣" 的讨论中给出有说服力的论点。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕职业发展。考官想了解你的职业抱负和对工作世界的看法。展示你的职业规划意识和对未来就业趋势的理解。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on career development. The examiner wants to understand your career aspirations and views on the working world. Show career planning awareness and understanding of future employment trends.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about the role of nature and the outdoors in your life.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your relationship with nature, outdoor activities, and the importance of green spaces. Give extended answers with personal examples.
examiner_questions: How much time do you spend outdoors? Would you like to spend more?
What outdoor activities do you enjoy? Why do you find them appealing?
Do you think people today are losing their connection with nature? Why might this be?
How important are parks and green spaces in cities?
If you could live anywhere in the world — in a city, by the sea, or in the mountains — where would you choose?
content.sample_answer: I probably don't spend as much time outdoors as I should, especially during term time when schoolwork takes up most of my day. I'd definitely like to spend more time in nature because I always feel refreshed and more positive after being outside. I particularly enjoy hiking and cycling because they allow me to explore beautiful landscapes while getting exercise at the same time. I do think many people, especially in urban areas, are losing their connection with nature. We spend so much time indoors, looking at screens, that we forget how restorative the natural world can be. Parks and green spaces are absolutely essential in cities — they provide places for exercise, relaxation, and social interaction, and they improve air quality and mental wellbeing. Studies consistently show that access to green spaces reduces stress and improves overall health. If I could live anywhere, I'd choose by the sea, because I find the sound of waves incredibly calming and I love the feeling of open space and fresh air.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于自然和户外活动的话题。技巧：1）诚实地反思你与自然的关系；2）描述户外活动时使用感官词汇（refreshed, restorative, calming）；3）讨论城市绿地时引用健康益处；4）使用高级词汇如 "restorative"、"consistently show" 展示语言水平；5）回答偏好问题时给出感性和理性的理由。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕自然和户外生活。考官想了解你与自然的关系和对绿色空间的看法。展示你对自然环境价值的理解和个人感受。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on nature and the outdoors. The examiner wants to understand your relationship with nature and views on green spaces. Show understanding of the value of natural environments and personal feelings.

content.stem: Personal Questions — The examiner will ask you about digital life, social media, and online identity.
content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about your digital habits, online presence, and views on the impact of social media on identity. Give thoughtful, extended responses.
examiner_questions: How much time do you spend on social media each day? Do you think this is too much?
Do you think people present an accurate picture of their lives on social media? Why or why not?
What are the advantages and disadvantages of having an online presence?
Do you think social media has changed the way young people see themselves?
If social media didn't exist, how do you think your life would be different?
content.sample_answer: I probably spend about two hours a day on social media, which I think is more than I'd like. I'm aware that much of that time is spent passively scrolling rather than doing anything productive, which can be frustrating. I definitely don't think people present an accurate picture of their lives online — most people share only their highlights and carefully curated moments, which creates a misleading impression of perfection. Having an online presence has clear advantages, such as staying connected with friends and accessing information, but it also comes with risks including privacy concerns and the pressure to maintain a certain image. Social media has undoubtedly changed how young people see themselves — the constant comparison with others can damage self-esteem and create unrealistic expectations about appearance, lifestyle, and success. If social media didn't exist, I think I would read more, spend more time with friends in person, and feel less anxious about what others think of me. However, I'd also miss the convenience of instant communication and the ability to discover new ideas and communities online.
content.tips_zh: FCE口语第一部分关于数字生活的话题。技巧：1）诚实地反思自己的社交媒体使用习惯；2）分析社交媒体上的形象与现实的差距（curated, misleading）；3）讨论在线存在的利弊时要全面；4）对社交媒体如何影响自我认知进行深入分析；5）使用虚拟语气回答假设问题（If social media didn't exist, I would...）。

explanation_zh: 这是FCE口语第一部分，话题围绕数字生活和在线身份。考官想了解你的数字习惯和对社交媒体影响自我认知的看法。展示你对数字文化的批判性理解和自我反思能力。
explanation_en: This is FCE Speaking Part 1 focusing on digital life and online identity. The examiner wants to understand your digital habits and views on social media's impact on self-perception. Show critical understanding of digital culture and self-reflection ability.
